summary: Coder v8 fixes all five v1 findings. F1 (blocking) is correct: strict (kind) rides with the value via app.data.IStrictKindEnforcer — an already-loaded/read-lift image.@this fails at the set, a lazy path-backed image throws StrictKindMismatchException at BytesAsync byte-load (Ingi's ruling), and ValidateKind now reads a loaded image instance, not only byte[]. Imprint survives storage (Variables.Set aliases by reference, verified by code-read). The new tests are proven non-vacuous by a mutation test: forcing CheckStrictKind to always-pass flips both ReadLiftImagePngAsImageGifStrict_FailsAtSet and BytesAsync_StrictKindMismatch_ThrowsAtLoad to failed, passing again on revert. F2 (Data.Kind -> [JsonIgnore], no duplicate wire kind), F3 (Scheme null-guard), F4 (text.Build deleted + gate removed), F5 (dead fast-path/wrapper removed) all clean. Build clean, C# 3815/3815, PLang 262/262. Deleted SetAsImageGifStrictRuntimeVarMismatch.test.goal (LLM build non-determinism) is acceptable — read-lift enforcement is now deterministically C#-covered; rewritten Mismatch goal has real assertions. Three minor non-blocking residuals (sync reads don't enforce; no full end-to-end lazy-chain test; imprint is process-local). Next: tester.
